questions.text: Gender inequality in any form is _ to the society
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Gender inequality in any form is detrimental to society. When individuals are not treated equally based on their gender, it leads to a multitude of negative consequences. It hampers social progress, limits economic potential, and perpetuates harmful stereotypes. Additionally, it denies individuals the opportunity to fully participate in society and reach their true potential, ultimately hindering overall societal development.

questions.text: Shylock the Jew lived at_
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Shylock the Jew lived in Venice, which was a city in northern Italy. "Paria" might refer to "pariah," which means an outcast or someone who is despised or rejected by society. In Shakespeare's play "The Merchant of Venice," Shylock faces discrimination and is treated as a pariah by some characters due to his religion and ethnicity.

questions.text: The first duty of the government is to maintain_
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The first duty of the government is to maintain law and order. This ensures the safety and security of its citizens, fosters a stable environment for economic and social development, and upholds the rule of law. Without effective maintenance of law and order, society faces chaos and instability, hindering progress and endangering the well-being of its members.

questions.text: The time for the _ of wounds has come.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The time for the healing of wounds has come. "Heeling" here refers to the process of wounds closing and mending, both physically and metaphorically. It suggests a period of reconciliation, forgiveness, and moving forward after a period of conflict or adversity.

questions.text: He that climbs too high is sure to fall the underlined is a
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The underlined phrase, "too high," functions as a prepositional phrase in the sentence. Prepositional phrases typically consist of a preposition (in this case, "too") and its object ("high"). Together, they provide additional information about location, time, or manner. In this sentence, "too high" modifies the verb "climbs," indicating the extent to which someone climbs.

questions.text: The Medicis were
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The Medicis were bankers. They were a prominent banking family in Florence, Italy, during the Renaissance period. Their banking activities contributed significantly to their wealth and influence, allowing them to become powerful patrons of the arts and influential figures in Florentine politics.

questions.text: The poem "IF" is composed by
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The poem "IF" was composed by Rudyard Kipling. It's one of his most famous works, known for its inspirational and moralistic themes, offering advice on how to navigate life's challenges with integrity, patience, and resilience. Kipling, a British author, wrote the poem in 1895 as part of his collection "Rewards and Fairies."

questions.text: Allah bas forbidden you to take _

questions.text: You are free to go to your _ or to any other place of worship in the state of Pakistan.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: "You are free to go to your mosques or to any other place of worship in the state of Pakistan." This statement highlights the religious freedom granted to citizens in Pakistan.

questions.text: It is obvious today that America has _ on his promissory note.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: "It is obvious today that America has deviated on its promissory note." questions.text: Pencillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in _
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Penicillin, the first antibiotic drug, was discovered by Scottish scientist Alexander Fleming in 1928. While conducting experiments at St. Mary's Hospital in London, Fleming noticed that a mold called Penicillium notatum inhibited the growth of bacteria in a culture plate. This observation led to the discovery of penicillin, which revolutionized medicine by providing an effective treatment for bacterial infections. Fleming's discovery laid the foundation for the development of many other antibiotics, saving countless lives and transforming healthcare practices worldwide.
